pages.raw_text:
Although Mayor Moore requested
that a representative of the Wash
ington Water Power Co. appear be.
fore the city council tonight rela.
tive to payment by the company OF
back cost of repairs due the city,
under the old Gable contract where
in the company agreed to pay half
the cost Of repairing Monroe st.
bridge, it is not believed the com
pany will comply.


1t is generally understood that
the w. w. P. Co., rather than per-
nit the construction of new
bridge from N. Monroe st. to Front
and Lincoln, will agree to pay at
least one half the cost Of the pro-
posed new structure for the old
site. Nothing definite regarding this
is known as yet.


Since the late rebuff given the
mayor and city officials by King"
Henry M. Richards, neither side
has made overtures. Peace repre-
sentatives, however, 1t is under
stood, will begin negotiations after
the council meeting tonight.


The Traction Co. is ready to step
in and pay a share of the construc-
ton of the proposed diagonal
bridge If the city will grant it a
franchise. Should this be the ulti-
mate outcome, the Traction Co.
would then have an opening to
build lines into territory now ex-
clusively served by the Washing
ton Water Power So.
